title: Gingered Korean Tofu Jjigae
description: A savory and bouncy Korean dish featuring green cardamom pods and nuka-pickle, perfect for a solo dinner with anti-inflammatory benefits.

ingredients.name: firm tofu
ingredients.notes: drained and cut into small cubes

instructions: Heat the sesame oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the grated ginger and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the crushed green cardamom pods and cook for another minute.
Add the gochujang, soy sauce, and korean chili flakes, and stir to combine.
Add the chopped nuka-pickle and cook for 2 minutes until the flavors are combined.
Add the cubed tofu and stir to coat with the sauce.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes until the tofu is cooked through.
Garnish with chopped green onions and toasted sesame seeds if desired.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
